steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question asking which choice best emphasizes the order in which the two films were released. The decisive clue is the goal word “emphasize” paired with “order” — the correct choice must make the release sequence the central point, not simply mention both films.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — *Alf's Button* (1930) was released early in Mira D'Souza's career, whereas *Forever and a Day* (1943) came out years later.: Uses temporal markers (“early” and “years later”) to place both films in sequence and make release order the main idea. ✓
  • Choice B — Early in her career, Mira D'Souza wasn't listed in some film credits, such as the credits for the film *Alf's Button*, where she played a nameless, uncredited role.: Discusses only one film and focuses on credit status, not release order. ✗
  • Choice C — Mira D'Souza was famous for her roles in films like *Alf's Button* (1930) and *Forever and a Day* (1943).: Mentions both films but frames them as examples of fame, not in any temporal sequence. ✗
  • Choice D — In *Alf's Button* (1930), actress Mira D'Souza played a nameless, uncredited role; however, in *Forever and a Day* (1943), she played a credited role—that of Marjorie Ismay.: Contrasts the type of role in each film (uncredited vs. credited), not their release order. ✗
That matches Choice A — *Alf's Button* (1930) was released early in Mira D'Souza's career, whereas *Forever and a Day* (1943) came out years later..
